info.title: verifiedsignal-api
info.description: VerifiedSignal HTTP API: session auth under **`/auth`** (Supabase-backed, including **`/auth/sync-identity`**); versioned resources under **`/api/v1`** (health, documents incl. signed original download, collections, users, search, SSE via Redis pub/sub). Bearer JWTs can auto-provision Postgres tenancy (users, org, inbox).

post.description:
Ensure Postgres `users` + personal org + `organization_members` + inbox `collections` exist.

Call after login if the SPA prefers an explicit hook; otherwise the first `Authorization`
request with auto-provision enabled already performs the same work.

schema.title: Response Auth Reset Password Auth Reset Password Post

get.description: List documents in collections the caller can access (org membership; optional dev fallback).

post.description:
Phase 1 intake: validate, insert canonical `documents` row (`created`), upload to S3/MinIO,
finalize to `queued` with `document_sources`, enqueue `process_document`.

Sync handler so `asyncio.run` inside storage/queue helpers is safe (runs on a worker thread).

Accept a remote URL: `created` row + `url` source, then worker fetch → S3 → pipeline.

Poll `GET /documents/{id}` for `queued` / `failed` after the fetch job completes.

get.description: Latest pipeline run and events for polling + UI progress (worker writes to Postgres).

post.description: Set the document's collection when both collections are visible to the caller.

Duplicate the document row (and sources + scores) into another collection.

Raw and extract object keys are reused until all rows referencing them are deleted.

get.description:
Download the stored original bytes (same access rules as GET /documents/{id}).

With real S3/MinIO, **redirect=true** avoids proxying large files through the API. Clients that
cannot follow redirects to the object host (or need a single authenticated hop) should use
**redirect=false**.

parameters.name: redirect

schema.description: When true (default), return 302 to a presigned GET URL if the storage backend supports signing. Otherwise stream bytes through this API (still requires Bearer auth).
schema.title: Redirect

parameters.description: When true (default), return 302 to a presigned GET URL if the storage backend supports signing. Otherwise stream bytes through this API (still requires Bearer auth).

302.description: Redirect to a short-lived presigned object URL (when supported).

404.description: Document not found, no original on file, or object missing.

502.description: Storage read failure when streaming the body.

get.description: Single document with intake sources (locators, mime, sizes).

delete.description: Delete canonical row (cascades sources) and remove raw object from storage when possible.

get.description:
Collections for the caller's org memberships.

Without a Postgres user row: if `VERIFIEDSIGNAL_ALLOW_DEFAULT_COLLECTION_FALLBACK` is true,
the seeded default inbox may appear (local dev). With auto-provision, the first Bearer request
creates a personal org + inbox.

post.description: Create a collection in an organization you belong to (or the dev default org when permitted).

get.description: Facet counts from the search index plus Postgres rollups on canonical `document_scores`.

patch.description: Rename a collection (slug is regenerated from the new name, unique per organization).

delete.description:
Remove a collection you can access.

Documents in this collection are removed (cascade); this cannot be undone.

parameters.name: collection_id

schema.description: Narrow to one collection (must be accessible when authenticated).
schema.title: Collection Id

parameters.description: Narrow to one collection (must be accessible when authenticated).

parameters.name: ingest_source

schema.description: Filter by primary ingest path: upload | url
schema.title: Ingest Source

parameters.description: Filter by primary ingest path: upload | url

parameters.name: tags

schema.description: Repeat for AND semantics, e.g. tag=a&tag=b
schema.title: Tags

parameters.description: Repeat for AND semantics, e.g. tag=a&tag=b

parameters.name: include_facets

schema.description: Include facet bucket counts (ingest_source, status, content_type, tags).
schema.title: Include Facets

parameters.description: Include facet bucket counts (ingest_source, status, content_type, tags).

get.description:
SSE endpoint. Clients receive ``connected`` then tenant-scoped events (``auth_sub`` on
payloads). Browsers should pass ``?access_token=<JWT>``; ``Authorization: Bearer`` also works.

parameters.name: access_token

schema.description: JWT for browser EventSource (cannot send Authorization header).
schema.title: Access Token

parameters.description: JWT for browser EventSource (cannot send Authorization header).

collection_id.title: Collection Id
collection_id.description: Target collection UUID; defaults to VERIFIEDSIGNAL_DEFAULT_COLLECTION_ID when omitted

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: Organization to create the collection in; omit for your primary workspace org

DocumentCollectionTargetIn.title: DocumentCollectionTargetIn
DocumentCollectionTargetIn.description: Target collection for move/copy; must be in the caller's accessible set.

body_text.title: Body Text
body_text.description: Plain text extracted for search (omitted in list responses).

canonical_score.description: Latest canonical row from document_scores when present.

IntakeResponse.title: IntakeResponse
IntakeResponse.description:
Response after successful file intake.

Row is `queued` once storage and DB finalize succeeded.

facets.title: Facets
facets.description: Facet buckets when include_facets=true (ingest_source, status, content_type, tags).

message.title: Message
message.default: Check your email to confirm your account

SyncIdentityOut.title: SyncIdentityOut
SyncIdentityOut.description: Result of `POST /auth/sync-identity` after ensuring Postgres rows exist.

collection_id.title: Collection Id
collection_id.description: Target collection UUID; defaults to VERIFIEDSIGNAL_DEFAULT_COLLECTION_ID

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional object (e.g. {"tags":["finance"],"label":"x"}) stored on the document.

UrlIntakeRequest.title: UrlIntakeRequest
UrlIntakeRequest.description: Remote URL; worker fetches bytes then matches multipart intake (storage + pipeline).

UrlIntakeResponse.title: UrlIntakeResponse
UrlIntakeResponse.description: Accepted: `created` until bytes land; then `queued` and `process_document`.

database_user_id.title: Database User Id
database_user_id.description: Primary key in Postgres `users` when provisioned or pre-seeded

email.title: Email
email.description: Email claim from the access token, if any

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Display name hint from token claims, if any

UserMeOut.title: UserMeOut
UserMeOut.description: Caller identity: Supabase `sub` and optional linked Postgres `users` row.
